content: A hybrid planning and learning system can be structured as a nested collective of semi-autonomous curiosity-driven micro-agents, where each micro-agent owns a specialized segment of the system's memory and actively seeks out novel data to refine its local planning module, rather than having a single centralized agent manage all memory and planning. This architecture leverages the hybrid system's core strength of combining structured planning with flexible learning, while harnessing distributed intrinsic motivation from multi-agent design to improve state-of-the-art active memory retention.
